fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: All routes start and finish at the Eschborn Ost event area, passing through Frankfurt before heading toward Oberursel and the Taunus. The Classic (97 km) crosses the Großer Feldberg, continues through Eppstein, Königstein and Kronberg, and adds the Mammolshainer Stich before returning to Eschborn; the Express (85 km) follows much of the same course but omits Mammolshain and the Königstein/Kronberg loop. It combines urban closed-road riding through Frankfurt, wooded Mittelgebirge climbing, fast descents and a lightly uphill finish back into Eschborn Ost.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Mammolshainer Stich — the Classic-only final major test, reaching a reported 23% maximum gradient, arriving after the Feldberg has already taken its toll.
suffering_zones: Großer Feldberg — roughly 11 km from Oberursel to the plateau, with a separately timed mountain classification
Eppstein — an old-town cobblestone section explicitly described by organizers as feared
The final approach into Eschborn Ost — widened but slightly uphill, a sting in the tail after 90+ km

logistics.lodging_strategy: Frankfurt or Eschborn hotels; start/finish is at the Eschborn Ost event area, not central Frankfurt.

history.origin_story: Runs alongside the professional Eschborn-Frankfurt WorldTour one-day race, sharing substantial course overlap with the pro route.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Record Sellout on Real WorldTour Roads — With Sloppy Route Bookkeeping
biased_opinion.summary: ADAC Velotour Eschborn-Frankfurt is a genuinely major German amateur sportive, running on substantial overlap with the professional Eschborn-Frankfurt WorldTour race and closing real roads to do it. The 2026 edition sold out a record 12,000-rider field nearly ten weeks before race day. What it doesn't have is stable published vitals: official materials gave conflicting route figures across the promotion cycle — 103/92/40 km early on versus roughly 97/85/42 km in the final event material — and the file previously carried a 104.6 km/2,000 m figure that matches neither.
strengths: Fully closed roads with substantial overlap with the professional WorldTour race
Three difficulty levels (97/85/42 km) plus a separately timed Feldberg mountain classification
Major-event atmosphere demonstrated by a record 12,000-place sellout nearly ten weeks early
Genuine Taunus climbing (Feldberg, Mammolshainer Stich) rather than a flat urban loop
weaknesses: Official materials gave conflicting route figures across the promotion cycle (103/92/40 km early vs. approximately 97/85/42 km final)
A 12,000-rider field creates real congestion risk on narrow Taunus roads despite staggered starts
Some route sections aren't normally open to bicycles, limiting riders' ability to pre-ride the full course legally
The Express is only about 12 km shorter than the Classic but skips Mammolshain and the Königstein/Kronberg loop — a materially easier ride than the distance gap suggests
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimately major event worth entering for the WorldTour road overlap and Feldberg climb alone — register early given the sellout history, and don't be surprised if the organizer's own distance figures shift as race day approaches.

final_verdict.one_liner: A major German amateur sportive on pro WorldTour roads with Taunus climbing — sells out fast, but the organizer's own route figures keep shifting.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a major, well-attended sportive on real WorldTour roads with genuine Taunus climbing. Register early — the 2026 edition sold out roughly ten weeks before race day.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similar pro-race-overlap amateur sportive, look at other WorldTour-adjacent European gran fondos run alongside their professional counterpart race.

citations.label: Official 2026 Race Newspaper — Final Route Figures

videos.title: Eschborn Frankfurt 2025 – Mein Erlebnis beim Radklassiker | 103 km & 1550 HM durch den Taunus!
videos.description:
Am 1. Mai 2025 war es soweit – ich habe am Radklassiker Eschborn-Frankfurt 2025 teilgenommen! In diesem Video nehme ich euch mit auf die komplette Strecke: 103 Kilometer und satte 1550 Höhenmeter durch Frankfurt, über den Feldberg, den berüchtigten Mammolshainer Stich und viele weitere Strecken-Highlights. 

"Bei strahlendem Sonnenschein durften die 10.000 Teilnehmenden heute am 1. Mai 2025 in die Pedale treten. "

Waren 10.000 :) 

💭 Da des öfteren der Einwand kam, die Strecken länger zu zeigen, habe ich diesmal bewusst das Video deutlich länger gelassen. Lasst es mich gerne in den Kommentaren wissen ob ich lieber nur bestimmte Segmente zeigen soll. 

🫶🏻 Meine Affiliates:

videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race experience video with detailed route coverage including specific climbs like Feldberg and Mammolshainer Stich.

videos.title: Skoda Velotour 2024 - Eschborn Frankfurt mit Data Overlay 40min highlight - raw footage, no comment
videos.description:
Die Skoda Velotour Classic Strecke über 103km im Rahmen des Radrennens Eschborn Frankfurt. Ein highlight jedes Jahr. Ich habe mein Ziel erreicht und bin sowohl über die gesamte Strecke als auch am Feldberg deutlich schneller gewesen als letztes Jahr mit fast 39km/h Durchschnittsgeschwindigkeit und 313Watt normalized Power. Am Feldberg konnte ich meine PB um fast 1 minute auf 28 Minuten verbessern mit 340 Watt im Schnitt verglichen mit 325 Watt im Vorjahr. 
Es gab in der Spitzengruppe trotz einiger haariger Situationen keine Stürze und wir sind alle gut durchgekommen. Ich würde mir trotzdem etwas mehr Vernunft und Rücksicht von manchen wünschen, wir fahren hier schließlich alle zum Spaß mit. 
Als nächstes Stehen für mich die Gran Fondo Vosges and und dann das RadRace 120 im Allgäu. 

PS: Es gab die ersten minuten Probleme mit mienem Herzfreuqenzmesser, nach 10-15 minuten hat er dann normal funktioniert :-)

Es kommt demnächst auch noch ein Video zu meiner Race Nutrition nach der ich schon von vielen gefragt wurde. 

Timestamps: 

00:00 Start mit Vollgas nach FFM
07:03 City Surfing durch Frankfurt
17:38 Oberursel Kopfsteinpflaster
18:25 Stimmung am Sandplacken
18:33 Frittziq mit Druck am Feldi 
18:52 Gipfelstimmung
19:04 Abfahrt Feldberg
22:29 Abfahrt Glashütten - Schlossborn
23:50 Abfahrt Elhalten
26:58 Mammolshainer Stich nach Kö
34:25 Schlussspurt ins Ziel nach Eschborn

eligibility.notes: Held 1 May 2026 alongside the professional Eschborn-Frankfurt WorldTour race; official results confirm route winners and a Feldberg climbing champion. Vitals corrected to Taunus Classic (97 km, just over 1,500 m) from the prior 104.6 km/2,000 m figure, which matched neither the early nor final published route data. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research as part of the Roadie Labs editorial wave 3.
